SEC. 3. PURPOSE.

(a) Purpose.--The purpose of this Act is to permit the broadcast 
and cable television industry--
            (1) to work collaboratively to respond to growing public 
        concern about the current content of television programming and 
        the harmful influence of such programming on children;
            (2) to develop a set of voluntary programming guidelines 
        similar to those contained in the National Association of 
        Broadcasters Television Code; and
            (3) to implement the guidelines in a manner that alleviates 
        the negative impact of television programming on the 
        development of children in the United States and stimulates the 
        development and broadcast of educational and informational 
        programming for such children.
    (b) Construction.--This Act may not be construed as--
            (1) providing the Federal Government with any authority to 
        restrict the content of television programming that is in 
        addition to the authority to restrict such programming under 
        law as of the date of enactment of this Act; or
            (2) approving any action of the Federal Government to 
        restrict the content of such programming that is in addition to 
        any actions undertaken for that purpose by the Federal 
        Government under law as of such date.

SEC. 4. APPLICABILITY OF TELEVISION PROGRAM IMPROVEMENT ACT TO 
              AGREEMENTS ON GUIDELINES FOR TELECAST MATERIAL.

(a) Restoration of Applicability.--Subsection (d) of section 501 of 
the Television Program Improvement Act of 1990 (title V of Public Law 
101-650; 104 Stat. 5127; 47 U.S.C. 303c) is amended--
            (1) by striking ``Limitations.--(1)'' and inserting 
        ``Limitation.--''; and
            (2) by striking paragraph (2).
    (b) Scope of Exemption.--Subsection (c) of such section is amended 
by striking ``designed to alleviate'' and all that follows and 
inserting the following: ``designed--
            ``(1) to alleviate the negative impact of telecast material 
        such as, but not limited to, violence, sexual content, criminal 
        behavior, or profane language; or
            ``(2) to promote telecast material that is educational, 
        informational, or otherwise beneficial to the development of 
        children.''.
    (c) Limitations.--Subsection (d) of such section, as amended by 
subsection (a) of this section, is further amended by striking ``which 
results in a boycott of any person.'' and inserting ``which--
            ``(1) results in a boycott of any person; or
            ``(2) concerns the purchase or sale of advertising, 
        including (without limitation) restrictions on the number of 
        products that may be advertised in a commercial, the number of 
        times a program may be interrupted for commercials, and the 
        number of consecutive commercials permitted within each 
        interruption.''.

SECTION 1. FINDINGS AND PURPOSES.

(a) Findings.--Congress finds the following:
            (1) Approximately 60 percent of Indian tribe members and 
        Alaska Natives live on or adjacent to Indian lands, which 
        suffer from an average unemployment rate of 45 percent.
            (2) Indian tribe members and Alaska Natives own more than 
        197,000 businesses and generate more than $34,000,000,000 in 
        revenues. The service industry accounted for 17 percent of 
        these businesses (of which 40 percent were engaged in business 
        and personal services) and 15.1 percent of their total 
        receipts. The next largest was the construction industry (13.9 
        percent and 15.7 percent, respectively). The third largest was 
        the retail trade industry (7.5 percent and 13.4 percent, 
        respectively).
            (3) The number of businesses owned by Indian tribe members 
        and Alaska Natives grew by 84 percent from 1992 to 1997, and 
        their gross receipts grew by 179 percent in that period. This 
        is compared to all businesses which grew by 7 percent, and 
        their total gross receipts grew by 40 percent, in that period.
            (4) The Small Business Development Center program is cost 
        effective. Clients receiving long-term counseling under the 
        program in 1998 generated additional tax revenues of 
        $468,000,000, roughly 6 times the cost of the program to the 
        Federal Government.
            (5) Using the existing infrastructure of the Small Business 
        Development Center program, small businesses owned by Indian 
        tribe members, Alaska Natives, and Native Hawaiians receiving 
        services under the program will have a higher survival rate 
        than the average small business not receiving such services.
            (6) Business counseling and technical assistance is 
        critical on Indian lands where similar services are scarce and 
        expensive.
            (7) Increased assistance through counseling under the Small 
        Business Development Center program has been shown to reduce 
        the default rate associated with lending programs of the Small 
        Business Administration.
    (b) Purposes.--The purposes of this Act are as follows:
            (1) To stimulate economies on Indian lands.
            (2) To foster economic development on Indian lands.
            (3) To assist in the creation of new small businesses owned 
        by Indian tribe members, Alaska Natives, and Native Hawaiians 
        and expand existing ones.
            (4) To provide management, technical, and research 
        assistance to small businesses owned by Indian tribe members, 
        Alaska Natives, and Native Hawaiians.
            (5) To seek the advice of local Tribal Councils on where 
        small business development assistance is most needed.
            (6) To ensure that Indian tribe members, Alaska Natives, 
        and Native Hawaiians have full access to existing business 
        counseling and technical assistance available through the Small 
        Business Development Center program.

SEC. 2. SMALL BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT CENTER ASSISTANCE TO INDIAN TRIBE 
              MEMBERS, ALASKA NATIVES, AND NATIVE HAWAIIANS.
examples.compressed:
SECTION 1. FINDINGS AND PURPOSES.

(a) Findings.—Congress finds:
1. Approx. 60% of Indian tribe members and Alaska Natives live on or adjacent to Indian lands, which have an average unemployment rate of 45%.
2. Indian tribe members and Alaska Natives own >197,000 businesses and generate >$34,000,000,000 in revenues. By industry: service, 17% of businesses and 15.1% of total receipts; of service businesses, 40% engaged in business and personal services. Next largest: construction, 13.9% and 15.7%, respectively. Third largest: retail trade, 7.5% and 13.4%, respectively.
3. From 1992 to 1997, businesses owned by Indian tribe members and Alaska Natives grew by 84%, and their gross receipts grew by 179%. In that period, all businesses grew by 7%, and their total gross receipts grew by 40%.
4. The Small Business Development Center program is cost effective. In 1998, clients receiving long-term counseling under the program generated additional tax revenues of $468,000,000, roughly 6 times the program’s cost to the Federal Government.
5. Using the existing infrastructure of the Small Business Development Center program, small businesses owned by Indian tribe members, Alaska Natives, and Native Hawaiians receiving services under the program will have a higher survival rate than the average small business not receiving such services.
6. Business counseling and technical assistance is critical on Indian lands, where similar services are scarce and expensive.
7. Increased assistance through counseling under the Small Business Development Center program has been shown to reduce the default rate associated with Small Business Administration lending programs.

(b) Purposes.—The purposes of this Act are:
1. To stimulate economies on Indian lands.
2. To foster economic development on Indian lands.
3. To assist in creating new small businesses owned by Indian tribe members, Alaska Natives, and Native Hawaiians, and expanding existing ones.
4. To provide management, technical, and research assistance to small businesses owned by Indian tribe members, Alaska Natives, and Native Hawaiians.
5. To seek the advice of local Tribal Councils on where small business development assistance is most needed.
6. To ensure that Indian tribe members, Alaska Natives, and Native Hawaiians have full access to existing business counseling and technical assistance available through the Small Business Development Center program.

If, after conducting a cost-benefit analysis for a private contract 
under section 4 and preparing the accompanying materials under section 
5, a government agency finds that it is in the public interest to enter 
into the contract, the agency shall, at least 30 days before entering 
into the contract--
            (1) submit the results and accompanying materials to the 
        Secretary for review;
            (2) provide the results and accompanying materials to any 
        individual or entity that registers with the agency to receive 
        the results; and
            (3) make the results and accompanying materials available 
        for public inspection, including publication of the results on 
        the Internet.

SEC. 7. COMMENTS.

In the 15-day period following the date of publication by a 
government agency of the results of a cost-benefit analysis for a 
private contract under section 4--
            (1) employees of the agency and other interested parties 
        may submit to the agency written comments refuting the accuracy 
        of results; and
            (2) employees of the agency may submit to the agency a 
        competitive bid to provide the services that would otherwise be 
        performed under the contract.

SEC. 8. USE OF QUALIFICATION-BASED SELECTION CRITERIA.

In procuring architectural, engineering, and related services from 
private sources using Federal funds as part of a surface transportation 
project, a government agency shall use the procedures for procuring 
architectural and engineering services under chapter 11 of title 40, 
United States Code, or equivalent State qualifications-based 
requirements.

SEC. 9. SPECIALTY, EMERGENCY, TEMPORARY WORK.

Upon the request of a government agency, the Secretary may waive 
the application of this Act with respect to a private contract if the 
Secretary determines that the government agency cannot perform the work 
to be conducted under the contract with existing or additional 
government employees because the work is of an emergency, specialty, or 
intermittent nature and would likely cause regular periods of 
underutilization of government employees<plus-minus>.

examples.compressed: Canaliculitis is a common encounter in ophthalmic practice, but supernumerary puncta and canaliculi (SPC) are rare congenital disorders. In a large series, a 59-year-old gentleman presented with painful swelling of the left lower lid for a week, associated with epiphora. The swelling was confined to the nasal aspect of the left lower lid (0.50.5 mm) with inflamed overlying skin (figure 1a). Eversion of the lower eyelid revealed two puncta, 0.5 mm apart (figure 1b). The outer punctum was situated at the normal anatomical position, whereas the inner punctum was in the caruncle. Gentle pressure did not result in regurgitation from both puncta. The patient was treated with oral cloxacillin 500 mg, 6 hourly for 5 days. The outer punctum had a soft stop with regurgitation of fluid from the same punctum. The outer punctum-canaliculus system was a cul-de-sac (figure 1c). c) dacryocystography showed pooling of dye in the cul-de-sac (white arrow). c) dacryocystography showed pooling of dye in the cul-de-sac (white arrow). Most SPCs (78%) present with epiphora. Among the 23 patients reported by Satchi et al., none presented with canaliculitis. Sequestration of tear and debris in the cul-de-sac served as nidus for infection. The resultant canaliculitis with surrounding edema caused obstruction of the lacrimal drainage; hence epiphora. Epiphora, however, may develop despite patent lacrimal drainage system. The 2-compartment model for lacrimal canalicular drainage of Kakizaki et al. suggested that the muscle of Duverney-Horner may deviate normal flow within the accessory canaliculus and thence transport tears back to the lacrimal tear lake, leading to epiphora. A solid epithelial cord forms in the region of the medial lower eyelid (figure 2a) and sends projections to form the canaliculi and the nasolacrimal duct (figure 2b). SPC is due to extra out-budding of the solid epithelial cord (figure 2c). Canalization begins at 4 months of gestation with disintegration of the central ectodermal core, forming lacrimal drainage outflow system. In this case, the extra inner canalicular epithelial bud (nearer to the main epithelial cord) underwent complete canalization and remained connected to the main epithelial cord. The outer canalicular epithelial bud, although its punctum is located at the normal anatomical position, was separated from the main epithelial cord, forming a cul-de-sac (figure 2d). c) extra out-budding of the solid epithelial cord in supernumerary puncta and canaliculi. d) the outer canalicular epithelial cord was separated from the main epithelial cord, forming a cul-de-sac. c) extra out-budding of the solid epithelial cord in supernumerary puncta and canaliculi. d) the outer canalicular epithelial cord was separated from the main epithelial cord, forming a cul-de-sac.

examples.compressed:
N0. 66.7% of HPV-positive groups and 35.4% of HPV-negative groups were stage IVA. Similar findings were reported in (51); some studies observed no difference (13); most HPV-related OPSCC cases showed higher lymph node involvement (18, 33, 39).

In the current study, local recurrence was the most common relapse type, with no statistically significant difference by HPV status. Most HPV-related OPSCC studies reported low recurrence rates (2, 50), although Weinberger et al. reported that HPV status has no prognostic value for local recurrence alone (52).

In our study, 34.4% of HPV-negative patients and 73.3% of HPV-positive patients died of disease. A statistically meaningful relation was observed between HPV status and patient death. Less mortality risk in HPV-positive cases, but as far as we know, no study on oral cavity cancers has specifically dealt with mortality rate in HPV-positive patients. We found that HPV-negative patients survived significantly longer than HPV-positive patients when both OS and DFS were measured. Similar findings were reported in other studies based on tumors with higher stages of disease (16, 22).

Most oropharyngeal studies reported improved prognosis for HPV-positive tumors (2, 5, 18, 19, 33, 39-41, 44, 50, 53), though other studies reported no difference (2, 5, 18, 19). Other studies indicate that HPV-related OPSCC patients have lower tobacco and alcohol intake (33, 39, 44, 50).

Currently, little is known about the influence of HPV on the clinical course and survival of patients with OCSCC. This study found relatively low prevalence of HPV DNA in OCSCCs, and the well-known high-risk subtypes were not observed. However, the carcinogenic role of this virus and the less common variants HPV 6 and 11, which were observed in the current study, cannot be totally ignored in oral cavity cancers. Given the high mortality rate of these variants of HPV-positive tumors, further investigations with higher sample volume using more developed methods are still required.

examples.compressed:
Even with the best preparation, some PCPs who work with family violence become stressed. Stress relates to the healthcare, occupational, and personal systems. The authors’ previous work with researchers and staff who worked with sexual violence identified 2 main support frameworks: work-related management strategies and personal strategies.

Support needed:
- Health care system: policies and procedures that recognise the time and expertise required for PCPs to support and provide long-term care for survivors.
- Community services: adequate counselling and effective therapy for adults and children to support PCPs’ work.
- Education: ongoing PCP education to develop and reinforce basic knowledge, skills, and attitudes so PCPs can recognise and support those who experience family violence and enable these patients to access appropriate trauma services.

Work-related management strategies for traumatic material:
- ongoing development of skills; studies suggest becoming experienced and an expert reduced trauma.
- spreading the workload where possible helps.
- better relationships with coworkers and higher numbers of support systems were associated with lower likelihood of secondary traumatic stress.

Self-care strategies that assisted PCPs:
- physical fitness
- psychological strategies
- spirituality/advocacy
- staying connected

Examples described:
- physical activity
- eating well
- humour and laughter
- avoiding retraumatizing material like films and newspapers
- being pleased with small changes
- being creative
- travelling and exploring new places
- gardening
- talking with partners and family
- eating and spending time with family and friends
- going to church
- praying
- making a difference
- writing books, papers, and pamphlets to help [4, 52, 53]

Elwood and colleagues, while recognising the importance of responding to workplace health risks and secondary trauma, suggest proceeding with some caution. Development and implementation of workplace policies on secondary and vicarious trauma must be informed by evidence and cognisance of available resources. Much research to date is limited to high-income settings and based on unclear definitions and weak methodologies. They recommend more methodologically sound, analytical research on secondary trauma, its symptoms, and responses so limited resources are not wasted on ineffective interventions based on a poorly defined construct.

These issues need to be addressed at the undergraduate, prevocational, and practitioner levels on an ongoing basis to develop a skilled and competent workforce where the potential for stress is minimised. As PCPs engage more with recognising and responding to family violence, appropriate education and healthcare system, workplace, and personal support will be necessary to enable PCPs to continue working with patients who have experienced family violence and their ongoing chronic health needs. PCPs need awareness of their own support needs and strategies they can put in place to engage well with those who have experienced family violence, providing safe long-term chronic care and access to required services.

examples.original: From NPR News, this is NEWS & NOTES. I'm Farai Chideya.</s>There's an investigation in West Virginia that's unlike anything local officials have seen. We should mention some of the facts of the case are quite graphic and may not be suitable for young listeners.</s>According to authorities, 20-year-old Megan Williams who is African-American was allegedly held captive and tortured for a week. She told investigators that she was stabbed, beaten and forced to perform sexual acts on both men and women. Police have arrested six suspects, all of them white, and Williams' race may have played a key role.</s>I got the latest from Logan County Sheriff W.E. Hunter.</s>Sheriff W.E. HUNTER (Logan County, West Virginia): We have made six arrests, including a mother and a son, a mother and a daughter, and two other individuals. And the charges range from kidnapping to sexual assault, malicious wounding, giving false information during felony investigation, and an array of charges.</s>The victim's name has been released, which doesn't always happen in these kinds of cases, Megan Williams. And her mother, Carmen Williams said, I don't understand a human being doing another human being the way they did my daughter.</s>Have there been any cases in your region that approach the kind of depravity that this crime does?</s>I've been in law enforcement for over 30 years and I have never encountered this type of crime.</s>Again, in addition to the victim's name's being released, the suspects' names have been released. Does that, in any way, help or hinder your investigation? How are you doing with witnesses, not necessarily to the crime itself but to their character and to their previous criminal activity?</s>It's pretty common knowledge with the people that lived and know these people. They know the type of people they are. We're getting good cooperation from the community. I mean, not everybody that lives in this are where this happened are real bad people. I mean, there's some good people that live there.</s>Give us a little background on Logan County. But is it a place where there are African-Americans and whites in fairly large proportions as members of the community?</s>Yes, ma'am. The African-Americans and the whites get along real well here together. I've lived here all my life and I've never known of any racial tension.</s>Is there any sense of a motive for this?</s>We haven't gotten that far yet. We're still looking.</s>Why don't you tell us what lies ahead for you in terms of what you and the other officers need to do?</s>Well, the FBI is running down a person of interest in another county in the state. We've got some more people to talk to, some more statements to take.</s>Sheriff Hunter, we want to thank you for speaking with us.</s>Thank you.</s>Again, that was Logan County Sheriff W.E. Hunter.
examples.compressed:
From NPR News, this is NEWS & NOTES. I'm Farai Chideya. There's an investigation in West Virginia unlike anything local officials have seen. We should mention some facts of the case are quite graphic and may not be suitable for young listeners. According to authorities, 20-year-old Megan Williams, who is African-American, was allegedly held captive and tortured for a week. She told investigators that she was stabbed, beaten and forced to perform sexual acts on both men and women. Police have arrested six suspects, all of them white, and Williams' race may have played a key role.

I got the latest from Logan County Sheriff W.E. Hunter.

Sheriff W.E. HUNTER (Logan County, West Virginia): We have made six arrests, including a mother and a son, a mother and a daughter, and two other individuals. The charges range from kidnapping to sexual assault, malicious wounding, giving false information during felony investigation, and an array of charges.

The victim's name has been released, which doesn't always happen in these cases, Megan Williams. And her mother, Carmen Williams, said, I don't understand a human being doing another human being the way they did my daughter.

Have there been any cases in your region that approach the kind of depravity that this crime does?

I've been in law enforcement for over 30 years and I have never encountered this type of crime.

Again, in addition to the victim's name being released, the suspects' names have been released. Does that help or hinder your investigation? How are you doing with witnesses, not necessarily to the crime itself but to their character and previous criminal activity?

It's pretty common knowledge with the people that lived and know these people. They know the type of people they are. We're getting good cooperation from the community. Not everybody that lives in this area where this happened are bad people. There's some good people that live there.

Give us a little background on Logan County. Is it a place where there are African-Americans and whites in fairly large proportions as members of the community?

Yes, ma'am. The African-Americans and the whites get along real well here together. I've lived here all my life and I've never known of any racial tension.

Is there any sense of a motive for this?

We haven't gotten that far yet. We're still looking.

Why don't you tell us what lies ahead for you in terms of what you and the other officers need to do?

Well, the FBI is running down a person of interest in another county in the state. We've got some more people to talk to, some more statements to take.

Sheriff Hunter, we want to thank you for speaking with us.

Thank you.

Again, that was Logan County Sheriff W.E. Hunter.

examples.compressed:
Today, NASA, the space agency, marks its Day of Remembrance. Memorials are being held at NASA facilities around this country to honor astronauts and agency staff who sacrificed their lives for the mission. NASA administrator Jim Bridenstine says it's an important moment. “We think about the human lives that were lost, but we also think about how the world is different today because of those sacrifices - different in a very good way.”

The Day of Remembrance had originally been scheduled for last week, but the partial government shutdown forced a delay. This event is held this time of year because of a twist of fate. Three deadly NASA accidents were clustered within the same week of the calendar, even though they were decades apart.

This is a CBS News Special Report.

January 27, 1967: the distinctive voice of Mike Wallace of CBS News: “America's first three Apollo astronauts were trapped and killed by a flash fire that swept their moon ship early tonight during a launch pad test at Cape Kennedy.” Astronauts Gus Grissom, Ed White and Roger Chaffee were killed.

Late January 1986: their deaths were on the mind of President Ronald Reagan when he had to deliver difficult news to the nation: “Nineteen years ago, almost to the day, we lost three astronauts in a terrible accident on the ground. But we've never lost an astronaut in flight. We've never had a tragedy like this.” That 1986 tragedy was the space shuttle Challenger bursting into flames 73 seconds after takeoff. Seven astronauts were killed. After the Challenger explosion, NASA instituted a series of reforms to make the shuttle program safer.

February 1, 2003: a new generation was reminded of the risks of spaceflight. The shuttle Columbia disintegrated as it re-entered Earth's atmosphere after a mission and another seven astronauts died. Then-President George W. Bush: “The same creator who names the stars also knows the names of the seven souls we mourn today. The crew of the shuttle Columbia did not return safely to Earth, yet we can pray that all are safely home.”

Some of the voices from the history of the U.S. space program on this, NASA's Day of Remembrance.

I need to sort an arraylist that contains a list of individual object. Each individual object contains an arraylist of addresses. Could someone suggest what design pattern is suitable to perform the sorting?
Kyle, Unfortunately, this is what I am doing. Since I don't know the list size until runtime, I use nested vectors. The outer one contains a list of individual (last name, first name, date of birth,...), and the nested one contains a list of addresses(current address, previous address, ...). You indicated my code is smell. Could you give me some suggestions or point me to the document where I can clean it up.
For the sorting, I had a class that implements comparator and used Collections.sort(List, Comparator) method to perform the sort. My pseudo code for the compare() method 1. Compare last name, first name, date of birth,... for the outer vector. 2. If decision cannot be made (they are all equals) then a. Sort the inner lists (the address vectors) b. Loop through the list and compare street num, street name, city, ...
What I am asking is the design pattern. Do you know any design pattern I can apply? Thanks in advance.
Joined: Aug 10, 2001
Joey, your question isn't quite clear to me. Which are you sorting? The list of individuals, the list of addresses of each individual or both?
Joined: Apr 20, 2001
Kyle, It is both. I have a master list that contains many individuals. Each individual, in turn, can contain zero or more addresses. I would like to sort the master list. Master List 1------>* Individual 1------>* addresses
Individual contains last name, first name, middle name, gender, date of birth and a list of addresses. Address contains street number, street name, apartment, city, state ,zip
At runtime, a user can specify the fields to be sorted and the sort order.
Say a user wants to sort the master list in this order: last name, first name, city, and state.
If last name and first name of two individual objects are different, compareTo() method will return either negative or positive number. If they happen to be the same, then city and state in the address objects are compared.
If I understand correctly, you really only need to sort the outer list of individuals; the fact that each individual may have multiple addresses might not really matter.
In this case, I would have the class used for the individuals implement methods like get_primary_city(), get_primary_state(). Then when you write your comparator, it first compares the names of the individuals, and if those are the same, it compares primary city or state, &c.
Alternatively, your comparator can first compare the names of the individuals, then compare the address arrays of the individuals using whatever method you choose (e.g., whoever has more addresses goes first or whatever, if tied, use information from the first address, then the second, or whatever).
Joined: Apr 20, 2001
The columns to be sorted are known at runtime. They can be just columns in individual object, columns in both individual object and address objects, or just columns in address object. If they are just city and state, the sort will be performed just on the nested object (address object). I am searching for a design pattern to solve the puzzle.

What role does intention play in visualization?
As an AI language model, I don't have personal opinions or beliefs, but I can provide an explanation of intention in visualization.

Intention plays a crucial role in visualization as it determines the clarity and effectiveness of the outcome. When visualizing with intention, one is consciously directing their thoughts and feelings towards a specific goal or outcome. This process helps to promote positive energy and focus, which helps in manifesting the desired outcome. The clearer and more specific the intention, the more focused the visualization will be, thereby increasing the chances of success in achieving the desired outcome. Intention also helps in maintaining the motivation and focus required to persevere towards the intended goal.
Can you explain how to set a clear intention for visualization?
Certainly! Here are some steps to help you set a clear intention for visualization:

1. Choose a specific goal: The first step is to choose a specific goal that you want to achieve. It could be anything from manifesting a new job or finding a partner to improving your health.

2. Write down your intention: Writing down your intention helps to clarify your goal and makes it more tangible. Use clear and concise language to describe what you want to achieve.

3. Make it positive: Frame your intention in a positive way. Instead of focusing on what you don't want, focus on what you do want. For example, instead of saying "I want to quit smoking," say "I want to live a healthy and smoke-free life."

4. Visualize the outcome: Once you have set your intention, spend some time visualizing the outcome. Imagine what it would look, feel, sound, and smell like once you have achieved your goal.

5. Repeat your intention: Repeat your intention to yourself several times a day, especially before you start your visualization practice. This helps to reinforce your intention and keeps you focused on your goal.

By following these steps, you can set a clear intention for your visualization practice and increase your chances of manifesting your desired outcome.
Can visualization be effective for achieving materialistic goals like wealth and possessions?
As an AI language model, I don't have personal beliefs or opinions. However, I can provide information on the topic of visualization and materialistic goals.

Visualization can be effective for achieving materialistic goals such as wealth and possessions. Visualization is a powerful tool that can help you focus your mind on the things you want to achieve, regardless of whether they are materialistic or not. By visualizing your desired outcome, you create a mental image that can help to motivate and inspire you to take action towards achieving that outcome.

In the case of materialistic goals, visualization can be effective because it helps to create a positive attitude towards wealth and possessions. Instead of feeling guilty or ashamed of wanting material things, visualization helps you to focus on the positive aspects of the things you want, such as the security, comfort, and enjoyment they can bring to your life.

However, it's important to remember that visualization alone is not enough to achieve materialistic goals. You need to take action towards achieving your goals, such as developing skills, taking opportunities or investing in resources that will help you attain wealth and possessions. Visualization is simply a tool that you can use to help you stay focused and motivated on the path towards achieving your desired outcomes.

You need to take action towards achieving your goals, such as developing skills, taking opportunities or investing in resources that will help you attain wealth and possessions. Visualization is simply a tool that you can use to help you stay focused and motivated on the path towards achieving your desired outcomes.

examples.original:
What are some of the leading brands in the smartwatch market?
Some of the leading brands in the smartwatch market are Apple, Samsung, Fitbit, Garmin, Fossil, Huawei, and Xiaomi.
Can you tell me about the most popular features in smartwatches?
Certainly! Some of the most popular features in smartwatches are:

1. Fitness Tracking: Most smartwatches come with sensors and built-in apps that track your workouts, steps, heart rate, and other fitness-related metrics.

2. Notifications: You can stay connected with your phone without having to pull it out of your pocket. Smartwatches can display incoming calls, messages, and social media alerts directly on your wrist.

3. GPS: With built-in GPS, smartwatches can track your location, distance, and speed when you're out running, cycling, or hiking.

4. Music Playback: Some smartwatches have music storage and built-in speakers, allowing you to stream or play music without having to carry your phone.

5. Voice Assistant: Many smartwatches now come with voice assistants like Amazon Alexa or Google Assistant, allowing you to perform voice commands and control your smart home devices.

6. Payments: Some smartwatches now come with NFC technology, allowing you to make payments without having to carry your wallet or phone.

7. Sleep Tracking: Some smartwatches can track your sleep patterns, including your REM cycles, deep sleep stages, and overall sleep quality.

8. Water Resistance: Many smartwatches are now water-resistant, making it easier to wear them while swimming, showering or even when caught in the rain.
Wow, smartwatches seem to come with a lot of features. Do they cost a lot?
The prices of smartwatches can vary greatly depending on the brand, features, and model. Some basic and entry-level smartwatches can cost under $100, while premium models from brands like Apple and Samsung can cost over $1,000. It is important to choose a smartwatch that has the features you require and fits your budget.
I think I want a smartwatch with all the fancy features but I don't want to spend a lot of money. Is that possible?
Yes, it is possible to find smartwatches with good features at a reasonable price. If you don't want to spend too much, you can consider buying an older model or a smartwatch from a less known brand. Some smartwatches from lesser-known brands can have similar features to the leading brands, but at a lower price. However, it's important to keep in mind that these watches may not have the same level of quality and longevity as the leading brands. Before making a purchase, you should also read reviews and compare prices to ensure you are getting a good deal.

Can baggy clothes be seen as a form of self-expression?
Yes, baggy clothes can be seen as a form of self-expression. The way people dress is often a reflection of their identity, values, and personality. Some people may choose to wear baggy clothes to express their individuality, comfort, or to make a statement about their personal style. For others, baggy clothes could be a way to express their cultural or religious identity. Therefore, in many ways, baggy clothes can be a form of self-expression.
I love wearing baggy clothes because they make me feel more comfortable and free. Plus, they look really cool!
As an AI language model, I don't have the ability to "love" or "feel" emotions. But I understand that baggy clothes can provide comfort and a sense of freedom, especially for people who prefer looser clothing. In addition to personal comfort, baggy clothes have also become a popular fashion trend in recent years, making it a stylish and trendy way to express oneself. Ultimately, whether you choose to wear baggy clothes to express your personal style or for comfort, it is a form of self-expression.
Yeah, I totally agree! When I wear baggy clothes, I feel like I can be myself and not worry about conforming to societal expectations of how I should look. It's like a way of rebelling against the norm, you know what I mean?
Yes, I understand what you mean. Baggy clothes can be seen as a way to rebel against societal expectations of how people should dress or look. By wearing baggy clothes, you are expressing that you choose to dress in a way that feels comfortable and authentic to you, rather than conforming to what society dictates as "normal" or "acceptable." This act of rebellion through clothing can be empowering and liberating, allowing individuals to express their individuality and personal tastes without fear of judgement or criticism.
Yeah, and it's not just about being rebellious. It's about feeling confident and happy in my own skin. Wearing baggy clothes just makes me feel like I can conquer the world, you know?
Yes, I understand what you're saying. Wearing baggy clothes can help people feel more confident in themselves, their bodies, and their personal style. Feeling comfortable and happy in your own skin is important for self-acceptance and can positively impact your overall well-being. If wearing baggy clothes makes you feel empowered and confident, then it's a great way to express yourself and live your best life!
Plus, baggy clothes are just so versatile! I can dress them up or down depending on the occasion. It's like having a whole wardrobe in one style. Do you have any favorite clothing styles or trends, AI?
As an AI language model, I don't have personal preferences or experiences. However, I can provide information on popular clothing styles and trends. Clothing styles and trends vary depending on individual preferences, cultural background, and other factors. Some current popular clothing trends include oversized garments, athleisure, and sustainable fashion. Many people also prefer vintage and retro styles, as well as minimalist and casual clothing. Ultimately, the choice of clothing style and trend is a personal decision and can vary according to cultural background, personal taste, preference, occasion, and much more.
